subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] x86/tdx: Fix zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Hansen expressed concern that the code is overly clever and may be unintelligible, suggesting a simpler approach using inline assembly to match the IN[BWL] behavior.

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dave Hansen suggested consolidating the patch by combining its logic with kvm's assign_register() function, citing excessive copy-pasting as a reason.
reviews.raw_body:
I think I could stand this if it consolidated this site with kvm's
assign_register(). The copy/paste is too much to bear.

reviews.author: David Laight
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David Laight expressed dissatisfaction with the verbosity of the patch, specifically mentioning that the use of GENMASK_ULL() macros makes it hard to follow.

Reviewer suggested replacing the switch statement in handle_in() with arithmetic code to make it more readable and concise.

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the TDX (Trusted Domain Extensions) code on x86 architecture, specifically for 32-bit port I/O operations. The issue was that the code did not properly handle zero-extension when reading from ports of different sizes. The patch introduces a switch statement to handle this correctly, ensuring that the result is written to the correct sub-register of RAX based on the size of the operation.
